examples.title: Creating a Shared Library
examples.description: Steps to create a shared library from source code
examples.explanation:
-fPIC generates Position Independent Code required for shared libraries
-shared flag tells gcc to create a shared library (.so) instead of executable

examples.title: Using Shared Library at Compile Time
examples.description: Linking a program with a shared library
examples.explanation:
-L specifies library search directory
-lfoo links against libfoo.so
-Wl,-rpath embeds runtime path in executable

examples.title: Dynamic Loading with dlopen API
examples.description: Explicit runtime library loading without compile-time linking
examples.explanation:
dlopen() loads library at runtime
dlsym() finds function address by name
dlclose() unloads library
RTLD_LAZY enables lazy binding

examples.title: Library Management Commands
examples.description: Common commands for managing shared libraries
examples.code:
# View library dependencies
ldd ./myprogram

# View symbols in library
nm -D libfoo.so

# List dynamic symbols
objdump -p libfoo.so | grep SONAME

# Update library cache
sudo ldconfig

# View library search path
ldconfig -p

# Check library type
file libfoo.so

# Strip debug symbols
strip -s libfoo.so -o libfoo_stripped.so
examples.explanation:
ldd shows what libraries a program needs
nm displays symbol table
ldconfig updates system library cache
strip reduces library size by removing debug info
